# Fix: Show "no checkpoint" indicator in diffview info buffer

## Context

The CKPT line in the diffview info buffer only appears for commits that have checkpoint data. On commits without checkpoints, the buffer is 2 lines (FROM/TO) and there's no indication the feature is even active. The user wants a visual indicator so they can tell the feature is working.

## File to modify

`~/neovim/lua/plugins/git.lua` â€” `show_commit_info_buffer()` function (line 218)

## Change

Currently (lines 245-249):
```lua
-- Add checkpoint line if available for TO commit
local ckpt = get_checkpoint_info(to_sha)
if ckpt then
    table.insert(lines, "  CKPT  " .. ckpt.tokens .. " tokens  " .. ckpt.tools .. "  " .. ckpt.summary)
end
```

Change to always show line 3, with dimmed "no checkpoint" when none exists:
```lua
-- Add checkpoint line (always show â€” indicator when no data)
local ckpt = get_checkpoint_info(to_sha)
if ckpt then
    table.insert(lines, "  CKPT  " .. ckpt.tokens .. " tokens  " .. ckpt.tools .. "  " .. ckpt.summary)
else
    table.insert(lines, "  CKPT  â€”")
end
```

And the highlight section (lines 278-282) â€” change from conditional to always-highlight:
```lua
-- Line 2: CKPT line (always present)
if ckpt then
    vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(commit_info_bufnr, ns, 2, 2, { end_col = 6, hl_group = "CommitInfoCheckpoint" })
    vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(commit_info_bufnr, ns, 2, 8, { end_col = #lines[3], hl_group = "Comment" })
else
    vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(commit_info_bufnr, ns, 2, 2, { end_col = 6, hl_group = "CommitInfoCheckpoint" })
    vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(commit_info_bufnr, ns, 2, 8, { end_col = #lines[3], hl_group = "NonText" })
end
```

Using `NonText` highlight group for the dash â€” it's very dim (almost invisible) in Tokyo Night, making it subtle but present.

The window height line at 297 (`vim.api.nvim_win_set_height(0, #lines)`) already uses `#lines` dynamically, so it will automatically be 3 lines always now.

## Verification

1. Open neovim in `~/dotfiles-entry`
2. Press `[r` to open diffview
3. On commits WITHOUT checkpoints: should see `CKPT  â€”` in dim text
4. Cycle with `[r` to `6fe40fb` (has checkpoint): should see `CKPT  4254 tokens  5 Bash  Agent session checkpoint` in blue
5. Both should have a 3-line info buffer

return {
		tokens = data.token_estimate or 0,
		tools = table.concat(tools, ", "),
		summary = (data.summary or ""):sub(1, 40),
	}
end

-- Checkout to FROM or TO commit
local function checkout_commit(which)
	local sha = which == "from" and commit_cycle_state.from_sha or commit_cycle_state.to_sha
	if not sha then
		vim.notify("No " .. which:upper() .. " commit to checkout", vim.log.levels.WARN)
		return
	end
	local result = vim.fn.system("MISE_QUIET=1 git checkout " .. sha .. " 2>&1")
	if vim.v.shell_error == 0 then
		vim.notify("Checked out to " .. sha:sub(1, 7), vim.log.levels.INFO)
	else
		vim.notify("Checkout failed: " .. result, vim.log.levels.ERROR)
	end
end

-- Show commit info in a split buffer below Diffview
local function show_commit_info_buffer(from_sha, from_msg, from_date, to_sha, to_msg, to_date)
	-- Create buffer if it doesn't exist or was deleted
	if not commit_info_bufnr or not vim.api.nvim_buf_is_valid(commit_info_bufnr) then
		commit_info_bufnr = vim.api.nvim_create_buf(false, true) -- nofile, scratch
		vim.bo[commit_info_bufnr].buftype = "nofile"
		vim.bo[commit_info_bufnr].bufhidden = "hide"
		vim.bo[commit_info_bufnr].swapfile = false
		pcall(vim.api.nvim_buf_set_name, commit_info_bufnr, "Commit Info")
		-- Add keymap to checkout commit on Enter
		vim.keymap.set("n", "<CR>", function()
			local line = vim.fn.line(".")
			checkout_commit(line == 1 and "from" or "to")
		end, { buffer = commit_info_bufnr, desc = "Checkout this commit" })
		-- Add keymap to close Diffview with q
		vim.keymap.set("n", "q", "<cmd>DiffviewClose<cr>", {
			buffer = commit_info_bufnr,
			desc = "Close Diffview",
		})
	end

	-- Format content with better layout
	local from_sha_short = from_sha:sub(1, 7)
	local to_sha_short = to_sha:sub(1, 7)
	local lines = {
		"  FROM  " .. from_sha_short .. "  " .. from_date .. "  " .. from_msg,
		"  TO    " .. to_sha_short .. "  " .. to_date .. "  " .. to_msg,
	}
	-- Add checkpoint line (always show â€” indicator when no data)
	local ckpt = get_checkpoint_info(to_sha)
	if ckpt then
		table.insert(lines, "  CKPT  " .. ckpt.tokens .. " tokens  " .. ckpt.tools .. "  " .. ckpt.summary)
	else
		table.insert(lines, "  CKPT  â€”")
	end
	vim.api.nvim_buf_set_lines(commit_info_bufnr, 0, -1, false, lines)

	-- Apply highlights using extmarks
	local ns = vim.api.nvim_create_namespace("commit_info_hl")
	vim.api.nvim_buf_clear_namespace(commit_info_bufnr, ns, 0, -1)

	-- Line 0: FROM - "  FROM  abc1234  2025-12-01 10:30:15 +0000  commit message"
	local from_date_start = 8 + #from_sha_short + 2
	local from_date_end = from_date_start + #from_date
	local from_msg_start = from_date_end + 2
	-- Get dynamic highlight groups for commit messages (persistent colors per SHA)
	local from_msg_hl = get_commit_hl_group(from_sha)
	local to_msg_hl = get_commit_hl_group(to_sha)

	vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(commit_info_bufnr, ns, 0, 2, { end_col = 6, hl_group = "CommitInfoFrom" })
	vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(commit_info_bufnr, ns, 0, 8, { end_col = 8 + #from_sha_short, hl_group = "CommitInfoSha" })
	vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(commit_info_bufnr, ns, 0, from_date_start, { end_col = from_date_end, hl_group = "CommitInfoDate" })
	vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(commit_info_bufnr, ns, 0, from_msg_start, { end_col = #lines[1], hl_group = from_msg_hl })

	-- Line 1: TO - "  TO    abc1234  2025-12-01 10:30:15 +0000  commit message"
	local to_date_start = 8 + #to_sha_short + 2
	local to_date_end = to_date_start + #to_date
	local to_msg_start = to_date_end + 2
	vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(commit_info_bufnr, ns, 1, 2, { end_col = 4, hl_group = "CommitInfoTo" })
	vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(commit_info_bufnr, ns, 1, 8, { end_col = 8 + #to_sha_short, hl_group = "CommitInfoSha" })
	vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(commit_info_bufnr, ns, 1, to_date_start, { end_col = to_date_end, hl_group = "CommitInfoDate" })
	vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(commit_info_bufnr, ns, 1, to_msg_start, { end_col = #lines[2], hl_group = to_msg_hl })

	-- Line 2: CKPT line (always present)
	if ckpt then
		vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(commit_info_bufnr, ns, 2, 2, { end_col = 6, hl_group = "CommitInfoCheckpoint" })
		vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(commit_info_bufnr, ns, 2, 8, { end_col = #lines[3], hl_group = "Comment" })
	else
		vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(commit_info_bufnr, ns, 2, 2, { end_col = 6, hl_group = "CommitInfoCheckpoint" })
		vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(commit_info_bufnr, ns, 2, 8, { end_col = #lines[3], hl_group = "NonText" })
	end

	-- Find or create window for the buffer
	local info_win = nil
	for _, win in ipairs(vim.api.nvim_list_wins()) do
		if vim.api.nvim_win_get_buf(win) == commit_info_bufnr then
			info_win = win
			break
		end
	end

	if info_win then
		-- Window exists â€” just resize to match new content
		vim.api.nvim_win_set_height(info_win, #lines)
	else
		-- Create horizontal split at bottom (2-3 lines depending on checkpoint)
		vim.cmd("botright split")
		vim.api.nvim_win_set_buf(0, commit_info_bufnr)
		vim.api.nvim_win_set_height(0, #lines)
		vim.wo[0].number = false
		vim.wo[0].relativenumber = false
		vim.wo[0].signcolumn = "no"
		vim.wo[0].cursorline = false
		vim.wo[0].winfixheight = true
		vim.wo[0].winhighlight = "Normal:NormalFloat" -- Use float background for subtle distinction
		-- Return to previous window (Diffview)
		vim.cmd("wincmd p")
	end

-- Commit info buffer highlight groups (Rose Pine theme)
local function setup_commit_info_highlights()
	vim.api.nvim_set_hl(0, "CommitInfoFrom", { fg = "#eb6f92", bold = true }) -- Rose Pine "love" (red)
	vim.api.nvim_set_hl(0, "CommitInfoTo", { fg = "#31748f", bold = true }) -- Rose Pine "pine" (blue)
	vim.api.nvim_set_hl(0, "CommitInfoSha", { fg = "#9ccfd8" }) -- Rose Pine "foam" (cyan)
	vim.api.nvim_set_hl(0, "CommitInfoDate", { fg = "#c4a7e7" }) -- Rose Pine "iris" (purple)
	vim.api.nvim_set_hl(0, "CommitInfoCheckpoint", { fg = "#7aa2f7", bold = true }) -- Tokyo Night blue
end

-- Set up highlights on load and colorscheme change
setup_commit_info_highlights()
vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd("ColorScheme", {
	pattern = "*",
	callback = setup_commit_info_highlights,
})

-- Look up checkpoint metadata for a commit SHA
local function get_checkpoint_info(sha)
	if not sha or #sha < 8 then
		return nil
	end
	-- Check if checkpoint branch exists
	local check = vim.fn.system("MISE_QUIET=1 git show-ref --quiet refs/heads/checkpoints/v1 2>/dev/null; echo $?")
	if vim.trim(check) ~= "0" then
		return nil
	end
	local shard = sha:sub(1, 2) .. "/" .. sha:sub(3, 8)
	local meta = vim.fn.system("MISE_QUIET=1 git show checkpoints/v1:" .. shard .. "/metadata.json 2>/dev/null")
	if meta == "" or meta:match("^fatal") then
		return nil
	end
	local ok, data = pcall(vim.fn.json_decode, meta)
	if not ok or type(data) ~= "table" then
		return nil
	end
	local tools = {}
	for _, t in ipairs(data.tool_calls_summary or {}) do
		table.insert(tools, vim.trim(t))
	end
	return {
		tokens = data.token_estimate or 0,
		tools = table.concat(tools, ", "),
		summary = (data.summary or ""):sub(1, 40),
	}

   786â†’					map("n", "<leader>hy", function()
   787â†’						-- Get the current hunk
   788â†’						local hunks = gs.get_hunks(bufnr)
   789â†’						if not hunks or #hunks == 0 then
   790â†’							vim.notify("No hunks found", vim.log.levels.WARN)
   791â†’							return
   792â†’						end
   793â†’
   794â†’						-- Find the hunk at cursor position
   795â†’						local cursor = vim.api.nvim_win_get_cursor(0)
   796â†’						local current_line = cursor[1]
   797â†’						local target_hunk = nil
   798â†’
   799â†’						for _, hunk in ipairs(hunks) do
   800â†’							-- Check if cursor is within this hunk's range
   801â†’							if current_line >= hunk.added.start and current_line <= (hunk.added.start + hunk.added.count) then
   802â†’								target_hunk = hunk
   803â†’								break
   804â†’							end
   805â†’						end
   806â†’
   807â†’						if not target_hunk then
   808â†’							vim.notify("No hunk at cursor position", vim.log.levels.WARN)
   809â†’							return
   810â†’						end
   811â†’
   812â†’						-- Extract deleted lines from the hunk
   813â†’						local deleted_lines = {}
   814â†’						if target_hunk.removed and target_hunk.removed.count > 0 then
   815â†’							-- Get the diff for this hunk
   816â†’							local diff_text = gs.get_hunks(bufnr, { greedy = false })
   817â†’
   818â†’							-- Get lines from git show for this hunk
   819â†’							local file_path = vim.api.nvim_buf_get_name(bufnr)
   820â†’							local git_cmd = string.format(
   821â†’								"git diff HEAD -- %s | awk '/^@@.*@@/{flag=1; next} flag && /^-/{print substr($0,2)}'",
   822â†’								vim.fn.shellescape(file_path)
   823â†’							)
   824â†’
   825â†’							local handle = io.popen(git_cmd)
   826â†’							if handle then
   827â†’								local result = handle:read("*a")
   828â†’								handle:close()
   829â†’
   830â†’								for line in result:gmatch("[^\r\n]+") do
   831â†’									table.insert(deleted_lines, line)
   832â†’								end
   833â†’							end
   834â†’						end
   835â†’
   836â†’						if #deleted_lines > 0 then
   837â†’							-- Join deleted lines and copy to clipboard
   838â†’							local content = table.concat(deleted_lines, "\n")
   839â†’							vim.fn.setreg('"', content)
   840â†’							vim.fn.setreg("+", content) -- Also copy to system clipboard
   841â†’							vim.notify(string.format("Yanked %d deleted line(s)", #deleted_lines), vim.log.levels.INFO)
   842â†’						else
   843â†’							vim.notify("No deleted lines in current hunk", vim.log.levels.WARN)
   844â†’						end
   845â†’					end, { desc = "Yank deleted lines from hunk" })

  1268â†’				-- Lifecycle and buffer hooks

  1270â†’					-- Called when diffview is opened
  1271â†’					view_opened = function(view)
  1272â†’						vim.notify("Diffview opened", vim.log.levels.DEBUG)
  1273â†’						-- Track whether we're in a merge state (to detect transitions)
  1274â†’						-- Uses get_git_dir() which handles worktrees (where .git is a file, not a dir)
  1275â†’						local git_path = get_git_dir()
  1276â†’						if git_path then
  1277â†’							view._was_merging = vim.uv.fs_stat(git_path .. "MERGE_HEAD") ~= nil
  1278â†’							local handle = vim.uv.new_fs_event()
  1279â†’							if handle then
  1280â†’								local debounce_timer = nil
  1281â†’								local reopening = false
  1282â†’								handle:start(git_path, { recursive = true }, function(err, filename, events)
  1283â†’									if err then return end
  1284â†’									vim.schedule(function()
  1285â†’										-- Debounce to avoid excessive refreshes
  1286â†’										if debounce_timer then
  1287â†’											debounce_timer:stop()
  1288â†’										end
  1289â†’										debounce_timer = vim.defer_fn(function()
  1290â†’											debounce_timer = nil
  1291â†’											if reopening then return end
  1292â†’
  1293â†’											-- Check if merge state changed (MERGE_HEAD appeared or disappeared)
  1294â†’											local is_merging = vim.uv.fs_stat(git_path .. "MERGE_HEAD") ~= nil
  1295â†’											if is_merging ~= view._was_merging then
  1296â†’												view._was_merging = is_merging
  1297â†’												-- Reopen Diffview to switch between normal and merge conflict view
  1298â†’												-- Stop watcher first to avoid callback-during-close issues
  1299â†’												reopening = true
  1300â†’												handle:stop()
  1301â†’												view._git_watcher = nil
  1302â†’												pcall(vim.cmd, "DiffviewClose")
  1303â†’												vim.defer_fn(function()
  1304â†’													pcall(vim.cmd, "DiffviewOpen")
  1305â†’													reopening = false
  1306â†’												end, 100)
  1307â†’												return
  1308â†’											end
  1309â†’
  1310â†’											-- Normal refresh for non-merge-state changes

  1314â†’												if current_view and current_view.update_files then
  1315â†’													current_view:update_files()
  1316â†’												end
  1317â†’											end
  1318â†’										end, 300)
  1319â†’									end)
  1320â†’								end)

  1323â†’							end
  1324â†’						end

  1326â†’						-- Cross-worktree merge detection: watch main repo's .git/ for MERGE_HEAD

  1341â†’									vim.schedule(function()

  1353â†’

  1367â†’												end, 100)
  1368â†’											elseif not main_merging and cross_worktree_state.active then

  1372â†’												cross_worktree_state.active = false
  1373â†’												cross_worktree_state.original_cwd = nil
  1374â†’												cross_worktree_state.main_work_dir = nil
  1375â†’												cross_worktree_state.main_git_dir = nil

  1379â†’												vim.defer_fn(function()
  1380â†’													pcall(vim.cmd, "DiffviewOpen")
  1381â†’													main_reopening = false
  1382â†’												end, 100)
  1383â†’											end
  1384â†’										end, 300)
  1385â†’									end)
  1386â†’								end)
  1387â†’								view._main_repo_watcher = main_handle
  1388â†’							end
  1389â†’						end

content.text:
Perfect. I have the complete picture. Let me provide you with the exact answers to your three questions.

## Summary: Commit Cycling in Diffview

### 1. How FROM and TO SHAs are determined

**They are ALWAYS adjacent commits** â€” TO is the current commit, FROM is its parent.

**Lines 475-497** (`show_single_commit_info` function):
```lua
-- Line 477: Get parent of current SHA
local parent_sha = vim.fn.system("MISE_QUIET=1 git rev-parse " .. sha .. "^ 2>/dev/null"):gsub("%s+", "")

-- Lines 489-496: Either set FROM to parent or to nil (root commit)
if parent_sha == "" or parent_sha:match("^fatal") then
    commit_cycle_state.from_sha = nil
    show_commit_info_buffer("(none)", "(root commit)", "", sha, current_msg, current_date)
else
    commit_cycle_state.from_sha = parent_sha
    get_commit_color(parent_sha)
    show_commit_info_buffer(parent_sha, parent_msg, parent_date, sha, current_msg, current_date)
end
```

**Key insight**: The range is always `parent^!` (lines 515), meaning commit compared to its parent only â€” never spanning multiple commits.

---

### 2. What is `commit_cycle_state` and how is it populated

**Definition** â€” Lines 98-106:
```lua
local commit_cycle_state = {
    current_sha = nil,              -- Current commit being viewed
    file_path = nil,                -- File path for file-scoped navigation
    from_sha = nil,                 -- Parent commit SHA (FROM)
    to_sha = nil,                   -- Current commit SHA (TO)
    sha_colors = {},                -- Map: SHA -> color index (for persistent colors)
    next_color_idx = 1,             -- Next color to assign from palette
    is_cycling = false,             -- Flag to preserve color state during cycling
}
```

**Population flow**:

1. **Line 508** (`open_commit_diff`): `commit_cycle_state.current_sha = sha`
2. **Line 511** (if file-scoped): `commit_cycle_state.file_path = file_path`
3. **Line 484** (`show_single_commit_info`): `commit_cycle_state.to_sha = sha`
4. **Lines 490 or 493**: `commit_cycle_state.from_sha = parent_sha` or `nil`
5. **Line 487, 494** (color assignment): `get_commit_color(sha)` and `get_commit_color(parent_sha)` populate `sha_colors` map

**Reset on Diffview close** â€” Lines 1407-1415:
```lua
commit_cycle_state.current_sha = nil
commit_cycle_state.file_path = nil
commit_cycle_state.from_sha = nil
commit_cycle_state.to_sha = nil
-- Only reset colors if user manually closed (not cycling)
if not commit_cycle_state.is_cycling then
    commit_cycle_state.sha_colors = {}
    commit_cycle_state.next_color_idx = 1
end
```

---

### 3. How `cycle_diffview_commits` works

There is **no function named `cycle_diffview_commits`**. The actual function is called **`cycle_commit`** (lines 522-554):

```lua
local function cycle_commit(direction, file_scoped)
    if not is_git_repo() then
        vim.notify("Not in a git repository", vim.log.levels.WARN)
        return
    end

    -- Line 529-542: Determine file_path (file-scoped or repo-scoped)
    local file_path = nil
    if file_scoped then
        local current_file = vim.fn.expand("%:p")
        if current_file == "" or current_file:match("^diffview://") or vim.bo.filetype == "DiffviewFiles" then
            file_path = get_diffview_current_file()
            if not file_path then
                file_path = commit_cycle_state.file_path  -- Fall back to stored path
            end
        else
            file_path = current_file
        end
    end

    -- Line 544-545: Find adjacent commit
    local current = commit_cycle_state.current_sha
    local next_sha = get_adjacent_commit(current, direction, file_path)

    -- Line 547-551: Boundary check
    if not next_sha then
        local msg = direction == "next" and "Already at latest commit" or "Already at oldest commit"
        vim.notify(msg, vim.log.levels.INFO)
        return
    end

    -- Line 553: Open the new diff
    open_commit_diff(next_sha, file_path)
end
```

**Keymappings** â€” Lines 557-568:
```lua
vim.keymap.set("n", "]r", function()
    cycle_commit("next", true)   -- File-scoped forward
end, { desc = "Next commit (file)" })

vim.keymap.set("n", "[r", function()
    cycle_commit("prev", true)   -- File-scoped backward (THIS IS YOUR [r)
end, { desc = "Prev commit (file)" })

vim.keymap.set("n", "]R", function()
    cycle_commit("next", false)  -- Repo-scoped forward
end, { desc = "Next commit (repo)" })

vim.keymap.set("n", "[R", function()
    cycle_commit("prev", false)  -- Repo-scoped backward
end, { desc = "Prev commit (repo)" })
```

**The workflow**:
1. `[r` calls `cycle_commit("prev", true)` with file-scoped navigation
2. `cycle_commit` calls `get_adjacent_commit(current_sha, "prev", file_path)` (lines 450-473)
3. `get_adjacent_commit` uses `get_commit_list(file_path)` to fetch all commits touching that file
4. It finds the next commit in that list, returns it
5. `cycle_commit` then calls `open_commit_diff(next_sha, file_path)` (line 553)
6. `open_commit_diff` (lines 500-519) opens `DiffviewOpen next_sha^!` and calls `show_single_commit_info(next_sha)`
7. `show_single_commit_info` resolves the parent SHA and populates the FROM/TO state, then calls `show_commit_info_buffer` to render the info panel

No â€” the current implementation only looks up the checkpoint for the **TO commit** specifically (line 246: `get_checkpoint_info(to_sha)`). And FROM/TO are always adjacent commits â€” TO is the commit being viewed, FROM is its direct parent (`git rev-parse SHA^`). ```

  TO    def5678  2025-12-01  commit message

The diff itself shows what changed from FROM to TO, so the reasoning behind that change is captured in TO's checkpoint. FROM's checkpoint would just be historical context that's already been applied, and adding it would mean extra shell calls and visual clutter without much benefit.

# Fix: Show "no checkpoint" indicator in diffview info buffer

## Context

The CKPT line in the diffview info buffer only appears for commits that have checkpoint data. On commits without checkpoints, the buffer is 2 lines (FROM/TO) and there's no indication the feature is even active. The user wants a visual indicator so they can tell the feature is working.

## File to modify

`~/neovim/lua/plugins/git.lua` â€” `show_commit_info_buffer()` function (line 218)

## Change

Currently (lines 245-249):
```lua
-- Add checkpoint line if available for TO commit
local ckpt = get_checkpoint_info(to_sha)
if ckpt then
    table.insert(lines, "  CKPT  " .. ckpt.tokens .. " tokens  " .. ckpt.tools .. "  " .. ckpt.summary)
end
```

Change to always show line 3, with dimmed "no checkpoint" when none exists:
```lua
-- Add checkpoint line (always show â€” indicator when no data)
local ckpt = get_checkpoint_info(to_sha)
if ckpt then
    table.insert(lines, "  CKPT  " .. ckpt.tokens .. " tokens  " .. ckpt.tools .. "  " .. ckpt.summary)
else
    table.insert(lines, "  CKPT  â€”")
end
```

And the highlight section (lines 278-282) â€” change from conditional to always-highlight:
```lua
-- Line 2: CKPT line (always present)
if ckpt then
    vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(commit_info_bufnr, ns, 2, 2, { end_col = 6, hl_group = "CommitInfoCheckpoint" })
    vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(commit_info_bufnr, ns, 2, 8, { end_col = #lines[3], hl_group = "Comment" })
else
    vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(commit_info_bufnr, ns, 2, 2, { end_col = 6, hl_group = "CommitInfoCheckpoint" })
    vim.api.nvim_buf_set_extmark(commit_info_bufnr, ns, 2, 8, { end_col = #lines[3], hl_group = "NonText" })
end
```

Using `NonText` highlight group for the dash â€” it's very dim (almost invisible) in Tokyo Night, making it subtle but present.

The window height line at 297 (`vim.api.nvim_win_set_height(0, #lines)`) already uses `#lines` dynamically, so it will automatically be 3 lines always now.

## Verification

1. Open neovim in `~/dotfiles-entry`
2. Press `[r` to open diffview
3. On commits WITHOUT checkpoints: should see `CKPT  â€”` in dim text
4. Cycle with `[r` to `6fe40fb` (has checkpoint): should see `CKPT  4254 tokens  5 Bash  Agent session checkpoint` in blue
5. Both should have a 3-line info buffer
